nodes.context: Neapolitan Camorra clan from Forcella, Naples, powerful from 1970s to mid-1980s.
Camorra organization headed by Raffaele Cutolo, rival to historic clans.
Camorra clan that proposed anti-Cutolo alliance with Giuliano family.
Alliance of Camorra clans formed to oppose NCO, including Giuliano, Zaza, Nuvoletta, Casalesi.
Camorra clan headed by Michele Zaza, member of Nuova Famiglia.
Camorra clan, member of Nuova Famiglia.
Camorra clan headed by Antonio Bardellino, member of Nuova Famiglia.
Camorra clan founded by Di Biasi brothers, took control of Quartieri Spagnoli from Giuliano.
Camorra clan, rivals and later allies of Giuliano clan.
Small groups aligned with Giuliano third generation, part of Paranza dei bambini.

edges.evidence_quote: The Giuliano clan had been in good terms with the Nuova Camorra Organizzata, headed by Raffaele Cutolo until the first half of 1979, but the two organizations then broke out into conflict.
edges.context: Giuliano clan was initially allied with NCO, then turned to conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: the head of the Vollaro clan, Luigi Vollaro raised the idea of an anti-Cutolo alliance with Giuliano family.
edges.context: Vollaro clan proposed an alliance with Giuliano clan against NCO.

edges.evidence_quote: The clash ... led to the formation of the Nuova Famiglia, consisting particularly of the Giuliano clan, the Zaza clan, then headed by Michele Zaza, the Nuvoletta clan and the Casalesi clan
edges.context: Giuliano clan was a founding member of the Nuova Famiglia alliance.

edges.evidence_quote: The clash ... led to the formation of the Nuova Famiglia, consisting particularly of the Giuliano clan, the Zaza clan, then headed by Michele Zaza, the Nuvoletta clan and the Casalesi clan
edges.context: Giuliano and Zaza clans were both members of the Nuova Famiglia alliance.

edges.evidence_quote: The clash ... led to the formation of the Nuova Famiglia, consisting particularly of the Giuliano clan, the Zaza clan, then headed by Michele Zaza, the Nuvoletta clan and the Casalesi clan
edges.context: Giuliano and Nuvoletta clans were both members of the Nuova Famiglia alliance.

edges.evidence_quote: The clash ... led to the formation of the Nuova Famiglia, consisting particularly of the Giuliano clan, the Zaza clan, then headed by Michele Zaza, the Nuvoletta clan and the Casalesi clan
edges.context: Giuliano and Casalesi clans were both members of the Nuova Famiglia alliance.

edges.evidence_quote: Luigi Giuliano relinquished control of the Quartieri Spagnoli to the Di Biasi brothers, who then founded the Di Biasi clan
edges.context: Giuliano clan ceded territory to Di Biasi brothers, leading to formation of Di Biasi clan.

edges.evidence_quote: the two organizations have always been rivals, which culminated in several episodes of violence in the 2000s.
edges.context: Giuliano and Mazzarella clans were rivals, with violence in the 2000s.

edges.evidence_quote: Together with elements of the Mazzarella clan, they allegedly formed a powerful criminal alliance aimed at regaining control over lucrative illegal activities
edges.context: Giuliano and Mazzarella clans formed a criminal alliance in the 2020s.

edges.evidence_quote: The third generation of the Giulianos along with other small groups aligned to them, such as the Amirante-Brunetti-Sibillo, is dubbed by the media 'Paranza dei bambini'.
edges.context: Amirante-Brunetti-Sibillo groups were aligned with Giuliano third generation.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Di Lauro clan, Vollaro clan, Rinaldi clan, Licciardi clan
edges.context: Di Lauro clan is listed as an ally of Giuliano clan in infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Di Lauro clan, Vollaro clan, Rinaldi clan, Licciardi clan
edges.context: Rinaldi clan is listed as an ally of Giuliano clan in infobox.
